mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real retail sales directly captures consumer spending momentum, which drives corporate revenue growth and pushes S&P 500 returns higher over the next month.
failure_condition: RSAFS 3-month change does not positively predict 20-day S&P 500 forward returns with Rank IC > 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer income expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and declining unemployment signals an overheating real economy that prompts anticipatory monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as discount rates reprice higher.
failure_condition: Joint z-scores of INDPRO growth and UNRATE decline do not negatively predict 60-day forward returns with Rank IC > 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, increases in the federal funds rate directly raise risk-free rates and equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns; this mechanism inverts during easing cycles.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not predominantly negative when FEDFUNDS > 2.0% and rising, or the mechanism produces positive returns in more than 50% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts reflects weakening residential investment and construction employment, foreshadowing broader demand contraction that leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month declines > 5% do not coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 forward returns in at least 55% of occurrences over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate operating leverage, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 3-month change in INDPRO fails to exhibit positive Rank IC with 20-day S&P 500 forward returns above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Stronger-than-expected real GDP growth revisions signal persistent aggregate demand strength that supports earnings forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth does not positively predict 60-day S&P 500 forward returns with Rank IC > 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment grows while unemployment simultaneously rises, the divergence signals labor market slack masking weak hiring quality, predicting negative S&P 500 returns as wage pressure and margin compression fears build.
failure_condition: The joint condition of PAYEMS increasing and UNRATE increasing does not produce negative 20-day S&P 500 forward returns in more than 55% of instances during walk-forward testing.
